title: Pathophysiology of Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS): The Role of Hyperinsulinemia
text: Polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS) is a heterogeneous endocrine disorder characterized by hyperandrogenism, ovulatory dysfunction, and polycystic ovarian morphology. Insulin resistance and compensatory hyperinsulinemia play a central pathophysiological role, driving ovarian androgen production and modulating gonadotropin-releasing hormone (GnRH) pulsatility.

title: Lean Phenotype of Polycystic Ovary Syndrome: Gonadotropin and Endocrine Axis Alterations
text: Lean patients with polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS) present a distinct clinical challenge. Unlike obese PCOS patients where insulin resistance is dominant, lean PCOS patients are characterized by hypothalamic-pituitary-ovarian axis hypersensitivity, featuring elevated LH/FSH ratios and accelerated GnRH pulse frequency.

title: Hyperandrogenism and Follicular Arrest in PCOS Patients
text: Hyperandrogenism is a diagnostic hallmark of polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS). Excess ovarian androgens impair follicular development, leading to premature follicular arrest, chronic anovulation, and the characteristic polycystic ovarian morphology visible on ultrasound.
